steps.title: Identify the verb tense needed
steps.content: Step 1: The independent clause after the verb (past event) requires a past-tense verb. The historians are noting an action Lincoln took in the past (1860 election, appointments), which is now being explained. The key is that Lincoln's action occurred before the present-day historians' evaluation.

steps.title: Apply the correct past-tense form
steps.content: Step 2: D uses the simple past tense 'leveraged' to align with the historical context and sequence of events. The other choices use present perfect ('has leveraged'), present tense ('is leveraging'), or future tense ('will leverage'), all of which incorrectly shift the timeline. That matches choice D — D is correct.
